report_name: Convincing Fake Microsoft Support Site Distributes Infostealer to French Users via Malvertising

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The campaign uses malvertising to direct users to a malicious typosquatted website, which is a form of delivering a malicious link to the user.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Users are socially engineered into downloading and executing a malicious Windows Installer (.msi) file disguised as a legitimate Windows update.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malicious installer uses the legitimate WiX Toolset framework and spoofs file properties to list 'Microsoft' as the author, in an attempt to evade detection and appear legitimate.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The infostealer payload is designed to harvest passwords, payment card details, and other sensitive credentials from sources like browsers and email clients on the victim's machine.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used a typosquatted domain `microsoft-update.support` and a pixel-perfect copy of a real Microsoft page to impersonate the legitimate vendor and build a false sense of trust.

malware.name: Infostealer
malware.description: A potent information-stealing malware payload designed to harvest passwords, payment card details, and other sensitive credentials from the victim's machine.

malware.name: WindowsUpdate 1.0.0.msi
malware.description: A trojanized Windows Installer package disguised as a cumulative update for Windows. It uses the WiX Toolset framework and delivers an infostealer payload.

tools.name: msiexec.exe
tools.description: The legitimate Windows Installer service used to execute the malicious `.msi` package on the victim's machine.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: The technology company impersonated by the attackers, who created a fake Microsoft support page to distribute malware.

identities.name: French-speaking Windows users
identities.description: The specific target demographic of the malvertising and phishing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Fake Microsoft Support Site
infrastructure.description: A malicious website hosted on the typosquatted domain `microsoft-update.support`, designed to impersonate an official Microsoft Windows update page and trick users into downloading malware.

indicators.name: Fake Microsoft Update Site Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Fake Microsoft Update Site DNS Query
id: 2b8c1d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to a malicious typosquatted domain impersonating a Microsoft update site.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/threat-intelligence/2026/04/this-fake-windows-support-website-delivers-password-stealing-malware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-09
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'microsoft-update.support'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network requests to the typosquatted domain `microsoft-update.support` used to host a fake Windows update page.

indicators.name: Malicious Windows Update Installer Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious Windows Update Installer Execution
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4b-4c3d-2e1f-0a9b8c7d6e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a malicious MSI file named 'WindowsUpdate 1.0.0.msi' via msiexec.exe.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/threat-intelligence/2026/04/this-fake-windows-support-website-delivers-password-stealing-malware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-09
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\msiexec.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'WindowsUpdate 1.0.0.msi'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the malicious 'WindowsUpdate 1.0.0.msi' installer file.
